title: Match the embedding to the query distribution
description: The embedding model and index are chosen for the actual query and document distribution, not a default, and re-evaluated when either shifts.
body: Use when selecting or changing the embedding model or vector index. Do not adopt a default embedding without measuring it on real queries, and avoid mixing embeddings from different models in one index. Actions: evaluate candidate embeddings on a labeled query set drawn from real traffic, pick the index type (flat, HNSW, IVF) for the corpus size and latency budget, and re-embed consistently when the model changes. Validation signals include a recall comparison across embedding choices on the labeled set. Risk boundary: a query/document distribution shift can silently degrade recall; route embedding changes through retrieval evaluation before rollout.
